Phase-27.NAME: Email Verification Foundation
Phase-27.Goal: Create the foundation for verified email registration so no KrotPN access is issued before email ownership is proven.

NonGoals.item-1: No MTProto provisioning in this phase.

Steps.step-1: Extend M-001 settings contract with email provider, verification TTL, allowed/blocked domains, and disposable-domain guard knobs.
Steps.step-2: Create M-040 email delivery adapter and verification templates using KPprotoN template lessons but KrotPN branding.
Steps.step-3: Create M-041 pending registration model/service with token hashing, TTL, replay protection, and normalized email storage.
Steps.step-4: Add email-risk checks: syntax, domain normalization, MX/DNS availability, configured disposable-domain denylist, and safe failure reasons.
Steps.step-5: Add focused backend tests and log markers without changing existing active /register behavior yet.

Verification.command: cd backend && PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_email_delivery.py tests/test_email_verification_guard.py -v
python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/modules/M-040.xml docs/modules/M-041.xml docs/verification/V-M-040.xml docs/verification/V-M-041.xml

AcceptanceCriteria.item-1: Email provider failures are typed and do not create user access.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-2: Verification token values are stored hashed or otherwise non-recoverable from logs.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-3: Temporary or configured blocked domains are rejected before sending verification email.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-4: The existing live registration behavior remains untouched until Phase-28 cutover.

Risks.risk-1: Over-aggressive temp-mail checks can block real users; allowlist override must exist.
Risks.risk-2: SMTP existence probing is intentionally avoided because it is unreliable and privacy-hostile.
